contactRoute: afar.com/about/pitch-guidelines — lists current section editors' pitch emails (travel news/trends editor); pitch the relevant one
notes: Pitch the data study as a travel-trends story: 'stars barely predict cosiness'. Favours fresh reported angles over roundups

contactRoute: No public inbox. Pitch the section editor (City Guides) via byline or Muck Rack; CN Traveller UK uses firstname.surname@condenast.co.uk
notes: Pitch a 'cosiest hotels in {city}' Cosy Index tie-in to city-guides desk; keep under 350 words

contactRoute: Public submissions inbox confirmed on their submissions page; section editors also listed
notes: Pitch 'best cosy hotels' rankings or the 1-in-150 finding as an under-the-radar trends story

contactRoute: Public general pitch address (confirmed on support pages); rarely on-spec, lead with the dataset
notes: Offer the dataset as backbone of a 'where the world's cosiest hotels cluster' piece

contactRoute: Pitch via their Google Form (linked from fodors.com/about-us); also published pitches@fodors.com but moved to the form
notes: 'Most-interesting' angle: the cosiest hotels in a city per the AI score; they like topical non-SEO hooks

contactRoute: thepointsguy.com/contact-us — no public pitch inbox; reach the hotels/loyalty editor via byline or Muck Rack
notes: Angle: which genuinely-cosy hotels are bookable on points; pair Cosy Index with their loyalty audience

contactRoute: No public inbox; pitch section editor via Muck Rack / Mediabistro 'How to Pitch BBC Travel'. Title <=39 chars, 50-100 word summary
notes: Pitch 'cosiness clusters in old, small, pre-car cities' as an unexpected shareable data feature

contactRoute: Public pitch inbox (Stories desk); reported to be pausing freelance pitches amid restructuring — verify before sending
notes: Quirky-data hook: 'only 1 in 150 hotels is genuinely cosy' as a curious narrative

contactRoute: Public editorial inbox confirmed on dwell.com 'How to Pitch' (design/interiors mag — pitch the cosy-design angle)
notes: The visual design cues that make a space read as cosy, drawn from the score's photo signals

contactRoute: No unsolicited pitches. Confidential tip form skift.com/share-a-confidential-tip-with-skift or pitch the hospitality reporter via Muck Rack
notes: Stars-vs-cosiness finding challenges hotel wisdom — Skift's sweet spot for industry data stories
Lead with the AI-scoring methodology; they cover travel-tech and data research heavily
Publish the hotelier 'make your listing look cosy' piece directly; large global hotelier reach
'Independents beat chains ~45% on cosiness' is tailor-made for boutique operators
Syndicate the data-study summary or hotelier asset as industry news

contactRoute: Exec Editor Alissa Ponchione listed at hospitalitydesign.com/contact-us
notes: Design angle: the visual cues that create perceived cosiness; pair with the 'look cosy online' asset
UK boutique/luxury hotel design audience; cosiness data + design narrative fits
Submit the hotelier asset directly as an insights article; 200k+ hospitality pros

contactRoute: Contacts directory ttgmedia.com/contacts (support@ttgmedia.com general); find news editor there or via Muck Rack
notes: The 1-in-150 finding is a strong hook for UK agents advising clients

contactRoute: No public inbox; pitch travel desk (Jane Dunford) or data team via Muck Rack; bylines use firstname.lastname@theguardian.com
notes: 'Only 1 in 150 hotels is genuinely cosy' is a strong consumer-interest data hook

contactRoute: Pitch form pudding.cool/pitch (reviewed monthly; freelance commissions year-round)
notes: 'What actually makes a hotel cosy?' — a culturally debatable, data-backed visual-essay premise
17k hotels scored = a natural shareable infographic (cosiness-vs-stars, independents-vs-chains)

contactRoute: Solo blog (Nathan Yau); contact link on flowingdata.com/about — flag the dataset/viz
notes: Pitch the cosiness-score distribution + methodology as a data-viz worth featuring/linking
'AI scores 17k hotels — stars are a poor predictor of cosiness' as a Smart Brevity scoop

contactRoute: Public Ideas pitch inbox, confirmed on Quartz's 'guide to writing for Quartz Ideas'
notes: Pitch the chain-vs-independent cosiness gap as an economics-of-hospitality angle

contactRoute: Email from 'Writing for The Agenda' page magazine.tablethotels.com/en/2016/08/writing-for-the-agenda
notes: Independent-hotel editorial; pitch the Cosy Index as a data companion to their curation
Hotel architecture/design platform; '17,727 hotels scored for cosiness — what the data reveals'

contactRoute: Editor Vicky Doe's email from hospitality-interiors.net/contact-us
notes: B2B hotelier/designer mag; 'how to make your hotel look cosy online' is a perfect practical feature
Anti-chain design-hotel discovery site; pitch the Cosy Score as a data layer atop their curation

contactRoute: Contact email on laurenbelz.com/contact (verify before sending)
notes: Boutique-hotel blogger who stays at every property; 'best cosy boutique hotels by city, AI-ranked' collab
Run by a hospitality interior designer; 'which visual hotel signals drive the highest cosiness scores'
UK's longest-running hygge blog; data study on what makes hotel spaces feel hygge is on-brand
Active UK cosy/hygge lifestyle blogger with TikTok; mirrors her pillars

outlet: How to Hygge the British Way
contactRoute: howtohyggethebritishway.com — no contact page; reach 'HyggeJem' via blog comments or her Facebook group
notes: Established UK hygge blog/author; a data-study guest post would suit her audience
Coastal + hygge lifestyle; 'best cosy coastal hotels, AI-ranked' natural listicle collab
Hygge small-space living; 'hotel design elements that score highest for cosiness — bring them home'

contactRoute: Email from slowtravelrepeat.com/contact (takes barter and paid collabs)
notes: Finnish slow-travel/wellness blogger; 'cosiest-scored hotels for a slow escape' rankings
Slow-travel blog since 2014 with a partnership page; 'cosiest cities for slow travel' angle
Copenhagen Scandinavian-design blog (hygge/lagom); '17k hotels scored for hygge'

contactRoute: Sign up free at helpareporter.com (revived Apr 2025)
notes: Answer travel/hotel-data journalist queries as an expert source; free 3x-daily digest
Curated Q&A model with 2,500+ media partners; answer travel/hotel questions for mentions + backlinks
Highest reported conversion of current platforms; register in travel/data verticals

contactRoute: Free signup at sourceofsources.com (Peter Shankman, original HARO founder)
notes: ~40k members, highest dofollow-link rate (~36%); strictly on-topic pitches

contactRoute: Subscribe to callouts at sourcebottle.com/subscribeProfile.asp (free to respond)
notes: Good travel/hospitality vertical coverage across English-speaking markets
UK-focused inbound journalist enquiries; worthwhile for UK travel/hospitality press

contactRoute: journolink.com (journalists post free; sources pay for PR access)
notes: 17k+ UK businesses; relevant for the UK hotel/hospitality angle
High-prestige outlets; pitch Got Cosy as a unique hotel-cosiness data source

contactRoute: Monitor x.com/search?q=%23JournoRequest and reply directly (no signup)
notes: Real-time journalist requests, very active among UK travel journalists
Flagship launch venue; free, no fee. Nofollow but huge referral+credibility. Launch Tue-Thu 12:01am PST. High effort.
Early-adopter angle. Free = 2-4mo queue, $129 skips it. Nofollow. Easy but slow.

contactRoute: Sign in > 'Suggest new application' at https://alternativeto.net (new accounts wait 1 week)
notes: List as alternative to Booking/hotel-finder tools. Free, high DR, nofollow. Low-medium difficulty.
Free listing, DR~74, 1-2 day approval, nofollow. Low difficulty.
Free submit + waitlist, DR~74, advertises a real SEO backlink. Good value, low difficulty.
Indie daily-launch feed. Free listing gives a dofollow backlink if you embed their badge. Low difficulty.
Chronological indie launch. Free dofollow if badge embedded, or ~$39 premium. Low difficulty.

contactRoute: Create profile at https://peerlist.io, launch via Launchpad (Mondays); guidelines https://help.peerlist.io/individual/launchpad/guidelines-faqs
notes: Free weekly launch, one/week, individual profiles only. Nofollow. Engaged builder audience.
Curated startup-tools directory, DR~68, free but selective. Evergreen backlink. Low-medium.
Large AI-tool directory. Free = 2-4wk review, ~$100 skip. Nofollow. Low difficulty.
AI directory actively taking submissions - easiest quick win; listings usually dofollow. Low difficulty.

contactRoute: https://theresanaiforthat.com/submit/ (paid ~$347) OR free via monthly free-submission thread on @theresanaiforthat (X)
notes: Largest AI directory, very high DR, nofollow. Free route is 1 tool/month via X thread. Skip paid unless budget allows.
Free daily-launch directory, product <1yr on own domain. Nofollow. Re-check signups reopened. Low difficulty.

contactRoute: https://news.ycombinator.com/submit - 'Show HN: Got Cosy - ...', URL in URL field, backstory in first comment. Rules: https://news.ycombinator.com/showhn.html
notes: Tool tryable without signup (qualifies). Never ask for upvotes. Nofollow but top referral spike. High value/variance.

contactRoute: Post a Product page + milestone at https://www.indiehackers.com/milestones
notes: Post the dataset origin story, not a bare link. Nofollow. Low difficulty.
Most self-promo-friendly sub; needs a story + feedback ask. Nofollow. Low difficulty.
Strong fit - bans signup-required products, Got Cosy needs none. Frame as fun unique web tool. Nofollow, strict mods, medium.

contactRoute: No link posts - answer questions genuinely or use weekly promo threads. https://www.reddit.com/r/solotravel/
notes: Strict anti-self-promo. Lead with dataset insights (cosiest hotels in X), not URL. Nofollow. High difficulty.

contactRoute: Answer relevant questions / post in a travel Space; policy https://help.quora.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043961972-Spaces-Policies
notes: Links only inside genuinely helpful answers; disclose affiliation. Nofollow. Build value first. Medium.
Large travel-stats hub that hyperlinks originals; pitch the cosiness stat. Public press email, high DR, likely dofollow. Medium.
Authoritative 2026 stats guide citing AHLA/STR/PwC with attribution. Strong topical+DR fit; pitch content/PR editor, no public email. Harder but high value.
Heavily-cited '100+ statistics' roundups that link to originals - classic link magnet. No submit form, general-contact pitch. Harder.
Current (Dec 2025) trend articles citing Deloitte/UNWTO. Pitch content team, no public email. Medium-hard, solid editorial value.
#1 global hospitality outlet; self-service submission = most reachable. Good authority, syndicated links may be nofollow.
Legit hotel-PMS trade blog; pitch the 'make your listing look cosy' guide. Dofollow author bio. Wants DA35+ reciprocal, 2000+ words. Medium.
Real hospitality-tech trade blog for the cosiness study. 1 dofollow link, 1000+ words, no fee (link-exchange). Legit, mid-value.
Established travel blog, travel-only, rejects junk. Fit for 'best cosy hotels for X' listicles; requires exclusivity. Medium.
Long-standing travel mag that PAYS writers, refuses link-grabs - high integrity. Caveat: currently not publishing new stories, slower now.

contactRoute: Article https://www.journohq.com/blog/best-ai-travel-tools-2026/ - pitch via About/contact + media kit
notes: Live 2026 roundup (Layla, Mindtrip, Wanderlog). Angle: Got Cosy as the cosiness-scoring layer. Medium; dofollow unknown.

outlet: LOCALS Insider - Best AI Travel Tools
notes: Live 2026 apps roundup; request inclusion as cosy-hotel ranking tool. Medium; contact route needs confirming.

contactRoute: Article https://www.afar.com/magazine/the-10-top-cozy-u-s-hotels-to-visit-this-winter - pitch editor via afar.com press/contact
notes: Directly on-topic, high authority. Offer Cosy Index data for next update. Hard; external links likely nofollow but strong brand mention.

contactRoute: Article https://www.timeout.com/usa/hotels/best-cozy-hotels-us - pitch via Time Out editorial/tips contact
notes: On-topic cozy-hotel editorial. Angle: Cosy Index as source. Hard; high-DR, likely nofollow, high brand value.
Active 2026, #1 hotel-industry show; contact page invites guest suggestions. Angle: cosiness study for hoteliers. Medium.
Active weekly, 418+ eps; public host email verified. Angle: solo founder building AI data product in public. Medium.
Active, top hospitality show. Angle: cosiness as a hotel differentiator. Medium; no public form.
Active into Season 7 (2026), #1 B2B travel podcast. Angle: AI + cosiness data trend. Medium-hard, exec-level guests.
Investor/operator travel-tech show that invites contributors. Strong AI/data-study fit. Medium; no public email.

contactRoute: Sign up as a Source at https://mentionmatch.com/ (helpab2bwriter.com now redirects here)
notes: Verified live + free. Skews B2B/SaaS/content-marketing - strong for AI-tool/dataset angle, weaker for pure consumer-travel.
Verified live + fully free (Wasabi Publicity). Experts get queries by email. Consumer mix fits travel; older, lower traffic.
Free tier aggregates #journorequest + sources; set keyword alerts (hotel/travel/cosy). Journalist contacts gated to paid; feed+alerts free.

contactRoute: Monitor #PRRequest (and #mediarequest); reply directly to the journalist
notes: Verified active, free. ~28 requests/day, UK-heavy lifestyle/travel/consumer - good hotel fit.

contactRoute: Monitor these hashtags on X/Bluesky/Instagram; reply to poster
notes: Verified active, free. Consumer/lifestyle/travel-blogger skew - strong for pure travel angles; more brand than expert quote.
